titleStmt.title: Letter from John Bernard Connolly to the Under Secretary of Ireland, 14 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Bernard Connolly

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Drogheda town clerk, John Connolly (1878-1919) to the under secretary of Ireland regarding Article 31 of the Schedule to the Local Government (Application of Enactments) Order, 1898. Connolly inquiries whether it is possible to have the number of members on the council altered. At the time this letter was written Sir Robert Chalmers (1858 - 1938) was serving as the under secretary of Ireland. Chalmers was sent to Ireland as a replacement for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5618/12108/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
TOWN CLERK'S OFFICE, DROGHEDA. The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle, DUBLIN. Sir Referring to Article 31 of the Schedule to the Local
Government (Application of Enactments) Order, 1898, which
provides for the alteration of the Wards of a Borough. I
beg to enquire whether it is competent for the Council of a
Borough by petition to the Lord Lieutenant to seek to have
the number of members of the Council altered? Section 26
of the Order referred to clearly vests in a County Council
such powers in relation to an Urban District, but it is not
so clear that the Lord Lieutenant has the same powers in
relation to a Borough. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant J.B. Connolly Assistant Under Secretary Submitted: I think
the Town Clerk may be informed
that his Council should be guided
in the matter to wh. he refers by
their legal advisors. The Solr General
should approve. W.B.C
18/2/16 I agree with Mr
Henry's suggestion: it would be a
mistake for us to advise every local body that
asks for advice. JOC 19.9.16
